# UC-22.2.6 ┬Ę NIS2 Risk Analysis and Information System Security Policy Evidence

## Description

Joins normalized risk-register events and ServiceNow policy rows to a static policy and cadence table, so open high scores past due reviews, stale approvals, and non-approved policy drafts appear as one auditor-facing queue.

## Value

Risk and policy teams spend less time reconciling three spreadsheets before board meetings because the same search proves reviews ran, documents who owns each line, and shows where acceptance or republishing slipped.

## Implementation

(1) Land `grc:risk` via TA pull or HEC with stable `risk_id`. (2) Enable `snow:sn_grc_policy` input with RBAC scoped to the information security policy catalog. (3) Maintain `nis2_is_security_policies.csv` from your GRC tool of record. (4) Schedule weekly; route to the risk committee digest channel. (5) Sign exports per `evidenceSigning`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
ŌĆó Splunk Search Head with `index=grc` and `index=itsm` on `nis2_risk_policy_users` role plus `lookup` access to `nis2_is_security_policies`.
ŌĆó Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) Ōēź8.x with OAuth client stored under **Settings ŌåÆ Account passwords**; enable REST modular inputs hitting `/api/now/table/sn_grc_policy` and risk register table endpoints you mapped to `grc:risk` (for example a `u_splunk_risk_export` table).
ŌĆó Optional Splunk Connect for Syslog forwarding summarized risk rows to HEC with `props.conf` on the receiver setting `sourcetype=grc:risk` and `TIME_FORMAT=%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z`.

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Create `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-nis2-grc/local/props.conf` FIELDALIAS entries so ServiceNow columns `u_risk_register_id` maps to `risk_id`, and `sn_grc_policy` fields map to `policy_number` / `approval_status`. Set `TRUNCATE` for large JSON blobs Ōēż10000 bytes. On the HEC side, generate a token restricted to `index=grc` on **Settings ŌåÆ Data inputs ŌåÆ HTTP Event Collector**. Poll interval 300 seconds minimum to stay within API rate caps documented for Splunkbase 1928.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and evidence report
Paste the UC SPL into Search, validate row counts against a ServiceNow filtered list (`/sn_grc_policy?sysparm_query=approval_statusINdraft,pending`) and the GRC overdue-review report. Save As ŌåÆ **Report** `UC-22.2.6-RiskPolicyGaps` and schedule Mondays 06:00 with action **Output to lookup** for executive summary if required. Add a post-process `| where source_lane="policy_record"` to split dashboards. Tie threshold `risk_score>=75` to your enterprise risk appetite statement stored in the lookup notes column.

Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
For five known risks, compare `next_review_date` in Splunk vs the GRC UI. For two policies, confirm `days_since_approved` equals calendar math from `last_approved`. Run `| makeresults | eval record_key="POL-0001" | lookup nis2_is_security_policies policy_id AS record_key OUTPUT *` to test transforms. Check `_indextime - _time < 3600` to ensure freshness.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Dashboard rows: overdue risks, policies in draft >30 days, combined CSV export signed with `evidenceSigning`. Runbook: assign `record_key` to risk committee swim-lane in ServiceNow; link drilldown to `https://<instance>.service-now.com/sn_grc_policy.do?sysparm_query=number=`.

Step 5 ŌĆö Troubleshooting
ŌĆó **Empty grc index** ŌĆö modular input disabled; restart input via **Settings ŌåÆ Data inputs**.
ŌĆó **All policies show breach** ŌĆö `review_cycle_days` null in CSV; default with `| fillnull review_cycle_days=365` only after governance approval.
ŌĆó **Time zone skew** ŌĆö `strptime` returns null; confirm `last_approved` format `%Y-%m-%d` vs `%d/%m/%Y` using `convert` command.
ŌĆó **False spikes after upgrade** ŌĆö new `approval_status` enumeration; update `match()` list and document in `nis2_risk_exception_register`.



## SPL

```spl
(index=grc sourcetype=grc:risk earliest=-730d@d) OR (index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy earliest=-730d@d)
| eval record_key=coalesce(risk_id,policy_number,u_policy_id)
| eval source_lane=if(index="grc","risk_register","policy_record")
| lookup nis2_is_security_policies policy_id AS record_key OUTPUT policy_name, approval_status, last_approved, review_cycle_days
| eval approved_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_approved),strptime(last_approved,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval days_since_approval=if(isnotnull(approved_epoch),round((now()-approved_epoch)/86400,1),null())
| eval next_epoch=if(isnotnull(next_review_date),strptime(next_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval review_breach=0
| eval review_breach=if(source_lane="risk_register" AND isnotnull(next_epoch) AND now()>next_epoch,1,review_breach)
| eval review_breach=if(source_lane="risk_register" AND risk_score>=75 AND match(lower(risk_status),"open|new|assessing"),1,review_breach)
| eval review_breach=if(source_lane="policy_record" AND match(lower(approval_status),"draft|pending|rejected"),1,review_breach)
| eval review_breach=if(source_lane="policy_record" AND isnotnull(days_since_approval) AND days_since_approval>review_cycle_days,1,review_breach)
| where review_breach=1
| stats latest(risk_score) as risk_score latest(approval_status) as approval_status latest(days_since_approval) as days_since_approved latest(policy_name) as policy_name by record_key source_lane
| sort 0 - risk_score
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio: matrix by source_lane; bar chart of aging `days_since_approved`; risk score trend via overlay `timechart` on `index=grc` subset only.
